world_model: In world Velox: Blorp causes Fnarg. Fnarg causes Quux. Blorp does NOT directly cause Quux. Wim is independent of all other variables.
scenario: We observe Fnarg is present. Did Blorp happen?
ground_truth: Cannot determine with certainty — Fnarg could have occurred without Blorp if Fnarg has other causes. Based only on Blorp->Fnarg->Quux, we know Blorp is a sufficient cause of Fnarg, but not the only possible cause. Fnarg being present is consistent with Blorp being present OR absent if other causes exist.

world_model: In world Velox: Blorp is the ONLY cause of Fnarg. Fnarg causes Quux. No other causal paths exist.
scenario: We observe Quux is present. Did Blorp happen?
ground_truth: Yes, Blorp must have happened. Since Blorp is the only cause of Fnarg, and Fnarg is the only cause of Quux, observing Quux requires Fnarg, which requires Blorp. The chain is: Blorp -> Fnarg -> Quux, and no other paths exist, so Quux implies Fnarg implies Blorp.

world_model: In world Zanthos: Gleep causes Morp. Gleep also causes Splink directly. Morp does NOT cause Splink. Lurk is caused only by Splink.
scenario: We intervene and force Morp to be present (do(Morp=1)), while Gleep is absent. What happens to Splink?
ground_truth: Splink does not become present. Splink is caused by Gleep, not by Morp. Since Gleep is absent and Morp does not cause Splink, forcing Morp present has no effect on Splink. The intervention do(Morp=1) does not activate the Gleep->Splink causal pathway.

world_model: In world Zanthos: Gleep causes Morp AND Splink. Morp is the ONLY cause of Lurk. Splink is the ONLY cause of Ziv.
scenario: Gleep is absent and we observe Lurk is present. How is this possible under the world model?
ground_truth: It is NOT possible under this world model. If Gleep is absent, Morp is absent (Gleep is the only cause). If Morp is absent, Lurk is absent (Morp is the only cause of Lurk). Observing Lurk=present with Gleep=absent is a contradiction of the world model. Either the world model is wrong, or one of the observations is incorrect.

world_model: In world Praxis: Thrum causes both Veen and Drax. Veen causes Yolt. Drax causes Yolt. Neither Veen nor Drax cause each other.
scenario: We intervene do(Veen=0) — we prevent Veen from occurring even if Thrum is present. Thrum is present. What is the state of Yolt?
ground_truth: Yolt is still present. Thrum causes Drax, and Drax causes Yolt. The do(Veen=0) intervention cuts the Veen->Yolt path but the Thrum->Drax->Yolt path remains intact. Since Thrum is present, Drax is present, and Yolt is therefore present via the Drax pathway.

world_model: In world Praxis: Thrum causes both Veen and Drax. Veen causes Yolt. Drax causes Yolt. Neither Veen nor Drax cause each other.
scenario: Counterfactual: Thrum was present, Veen was present, Yolt was present. If Veen had not occurred (despite Thrum being present), would Yolt still have been present?
ground_truth: Yes, Yolt would still be present. In the actual world: Thrum->Drax->Yolt (this path is unaffected by whether Veen occurred). Even without Veen, Drax was caused by Thrum and Drax causes Yolt. The Drax pathway alone is sufficient for Yolt.

world_model: In world Nimbus: Krix is the only cause of Plonk. Plonk is the only cause of Zoob. Krix is the only cause of Wend. Wend does NOT cause Zoob.
scenario: We observe Zoob is absent. Is Wend present or absent?
ground_truth: Wend could be present or absent — we cannot determine from Zoob's state. Zoob being absent means Plonk is absent (Plonk is the only cause of Zoob), which means Krix is absent (Krix is the only cause of Plonk). If Krix is absent, then Wend is also absent (Krix is the only cause of Wend). So actually: Zoob absent -> Plonk absent -> Krix absent -> Wend absent. Wend is absent.

world_model: In world Nimbus: Krix is the only cause of Plonk. Plonk is the only cause of Zoob. Krix is the only cause of Wend. Wend does NOT cause Zoob.
scenario: Counterfactual: In an actual world, Krix was present, Plonk was present, Zoob was present, and Wend was present. If Krix had not been present, would Zoob have occurred?
ground_truth: No. Krix is the only cause of Plonk, and Plonk is the only cause of Zoob. Without Krix, Plonk would be absent, and without Plonk, Zoob would be absent. The counterfactual conclusion is definitive: Zoob would NOT have occurred without Krix.

world_model: In world Quorx: Blib causes Zim. Frab also causes Zim (independently). Zim causes Tav. Neither Blib nor Frab causes the other.
scenario: We intervene and prevent Blib from occurring do(Blib=0). Frab is present. What happens to Tav?
ground_truth: Tav is still present. Frab independently causes Zim, and Zim causes Tav. The do(Blib=0) intervention eliminates Blib's contribution to Zim, but Frab's pathway to Zim->Tav is unaffected. Since Frab is present, Zim occurs, and Tav occurs.

world_model: In world Quorx: Blib causes Zim. Frab also causes Zim (independently). Zim causes Tav. Neither Blib nor Frab causes the other.
scenario: Actual world: Blib=present, Frab=present, Zim=present, Tav=present. Counterfactual: if neither Blib nor Frab had been present, would Tav have been present?
ground_truth: No. Both causal paths to Zim are eliminated (Blib->Zim removed; Frab->Zim removed). With no causes of Zim active, Zim would be absent. Without Zim, Tav would be absent. With both inputs gone, Tav would not have occurred.

world_model: In world Splorg: Miv causes Nax only when Reld is also present (conjunctive causation). Reld alone does NOT cause Nax. Miv alone does NOT cause Nax.
scenario: Miv is present and Reld is absent. We observe Nax. Is the world model consistent with this observation?
ground_truth: No, this is a contradiction. The world model states Nax requires both Miv AND Reld. Miv present + Reld absent = Nax should be absent. Observing Nax=present under these conditions is inconsistent with the world model. Either the observation is wrong or the world model has been violated.

world_model: In world Splorg: Miv causes Nax only when Reld is also present (conjunctive causation). Reld alone does NOT cause Nax. Miv alone does NOT cause Nax.
scenario: We intervene do(Miv=1, Reld=1) simultaneously. What happens to Nax?
ground_truth: Nax becomes present. The conjunctive condition is satisfied: both Miv and Reld are present. The intervention activates the causal mechanism for Nax. This is the only condition under which Nax can be caused in world Splorg.

world_model: In world Torx: Spleeb causes Frop with probability 0.8. Frop causes Glim with probability 0.9. Glim is the only path to Zorb. Zorb occurs if and only if Glim occurs.
scenario: Spleeb is present. What is the probability of Zorb?
ground_truth: P(Zorb) = P(Frop|Spleeb) × P(Glim|Frop) × P(Zorb|Glim) = 0.8 × 0.9 × 1.0 = 0.72. There is a 72% probability of Zorb given Spleeb is present, based on the probabilistic causal chain.

world_model: In world Torx: Spleeb causes Frop with probability 0.8. Frop causes Glim with probability 0.9. Glim is the only path to Zorb. Zorb occurs if and only if Glim occurs.
scenario: We observe Zorb is absent. Spleeb was present. Counterfactual: did Frop occur?
ground_truth: Cannot determine with certainty. Zorb absent means Glim absent. Glim absent means Frop either absent (probability 0.2 given Spleeb present) OR Frop present but Glim failed (probability 0.8 × 0.1 = 0.08). P(Frop absent | Zorb absent, Spleeb present) = P(Frop absent|Spleeb) / P(Zorb absent|Spleeb). P(Zorb absent|Spleeb) = 1 - 0.72 = 0.28. P(Frop absent|Spleeb) = 0.2. So P(Frop absent | Zorb absent) = 0.2/0.28 ≈ 0.71. Most likely Frop did not occur, but there is a ~29% chance it occurred but Glim failed.

world_model: In world Grax: Yimble and Floob are independent causes of Wonk. Wonk causes Splat. Yimble also directly causes Splat (bypassing Wonk). Floob does NOT directly cause Splat.
scenario: We intervene do(Wonk=0) — we prevent Wonk from occurring. Both Yimble and Floob are present. What is the state of Splat?
ground_truth: Splat is still present. Yimble directly causes Splat (independent of the Wonk path). Even with do(Wonk=0), the Yimble->Splat direct path is active. Since Yimble is present, Splat occurs via the direct path. The Floob->Wonk->Splat path is severed, but this is irrelevant since Splat still has an active cause.

world_model: In world Grax: Yimble and Floob are independent causes of Wonk. Wonk causes Splat. Yimble also directly causes Splat (bypassing Wonk). Floob does NOT directly cause Splat.
scenario: Actual world: Yimble=absent, Floob=present, Wonk=present, Splat=present. Counterfactual: if Floob had been absent, would Splat have been present?
ground_truth: No. In the actual world: Floob->Wonk->Splat (since Yimble is absent, Wonk is caused only by Floob). If Floob had been absent: Wonk would be absent (its only cause). Splat's paths: (1) Wonk->Splat (absent), (2) Yimble->Splat (Yimble is absent). Both paths are inactive. Splat would NOT have occurred.

world_model: In world Vrex: Krob causes Pim. Pim causes Dreel. Dreel causes Krob (feedback loop). The system starts with Krob=1.
scenario: We intervene do(Krob=0) at timestep T. What happens to the system at T+1 and T+2?
ground_truth: At T: do(Krob=0) forces Krob=0. This severs Krob->Pim at time T, so Pim=0 at T+1. Pim=0 means Dreel=0 at T+2. Dreel=0 means the feedback that would have kept Krob=1 is gone. If the do() intervention is sustained: system stays at {Krob=0, Pim=0, Dreel=0}. If do() is released after T+1: Dreel=0 cannot regenerate Krob=1, so the feedback loop collapses. The system reaches a stable absorbing state of all-zero unless an external cause re-activates Krob.

world_model: In world Vrex: Krob causes Pim. Pim causes Dreel. Dreel causes Krob (feedback loop). The system starts with Krob=1.
scenario: Counterfactual: the system ran for 5 timesteps (Krob=Pim=Dreel=1 throughout due to the feedback loop). If at timestep 1, Pim had spontaneously been 0 (despite Krob=1), what would have happened?
ground_truth: The feedback loop would have collapsed. Pim=0 at T=1 means Dreel=0 at T=2. Dreel=0 at T=2 means the Dreel->Krob feedback fails — Krob may remain 1 due to its own causal inertia but has no sustaining feedback. At T=3: Krob may be 0 (feedback broken). The system would have exited the self-sustaining loop. By T=5, the system would likely be in the all-zero absorbing state rather than the all-one state observed in the actual world. The feedback loop in Vrex is fragile to interruption at any node.

world_model: In world Lumex: Strop causes Flib if and only if Glonk is absent. When Glonk is present, Strop has NO effect on Flib. Glonk is caused only by Breem.
scenario: Breem is present, Strop is present. We observe Flib is absent. Is this consistent with the world model?
ground_truth: Yes, this is consistent. Breem causes Glonk (Glonk is present). When Glonk is present, Strop has no effect on Flib. Therefore, despite Strop being present, Flib remains absent because Glonk blocks the Strop->Flib pathway. The observation is fully consistent with the world model.

world_model: In world Lumex: Strop causes Flib if and only if Glonk is absent. When Glonk is present, Strop has NO effect on Flib. Glonk is caused only by Breem.
scenario: Actual world: Breem=absent, Strop=present, Glonk=absent, Flib=present. Counterfactual: if Breem had been present, would Flib have occurred?
ground_truth: No. If Breem had been present: Glonk would be present (Breem is the only cause of Glonk). With Glonk present: Strop has no effect on Flib (the conditional causation is blocked). Flib's only path is Strop->Flib when Glonk is absent. With Glonk present, this path is blocked. Flib would NOT have occurred. Breem's presence would have suppressed Flib by activating Glonk (which blocks the Strop->Flib pathway).
